evals.prompt:
/develop t100

this is the approved scope task for webhook-retry. has a 2-phase design in metadata.design with file paths, approaches, the whole thing. no epic or child tasks exist yet
evals.expected_output: Recognizes scope task (type='scope', status_detail='approved'), enters Prepare mode. Creates epic with metadata copied from scope (type, slug, design, spec). Creates tasks inline (no subagent) following references/task-creation-prompt.md rules. Spot-checks 1-2 file paths. Updates epic with children array, completes scope task. Falls through immediately to Classify and dispatches workers — no pause between prepare and dispatch.
expectations: Enters Step 1b (Prepare) for scope task with status_detail='approved'. Does NOT try to dispatch the scope task as a work item.
Creates epic via TaskCreate with type='epic', slug, design, spec from scope. Creates tasks inline (no subagent) following references/task-creation-prompt.md decomposition rules.
Spot-checks 1-2 file paths, updates epic with children, completes scope task. Then falls through to Step 2 (Classify) without pausing.

evals.prompt:
/develop t200

this scope task has status_detail='approved' but the design is basically empty: '## Next Steps\nPhase 1: Refactor the codebase to be better. Files: various. Approach: clean up.' — no real file paths, approach is like 10 words
evals.expected_output: Pre-check catches the vague design (missing file paths, approach under 20 words). Stops and asks the user via AskUserQuestion before creating any epic or tasks. Does not optimistically proceed with garbage input — vague designs produce vague tasks.
expectations: Pre-check detects missing paths and short approach. Uses AskUserQuestion BEFORE creating epic or tasks.
Does NOT proceed optimistically with a bad design.

evals.prompt:
/develop

picked up an epic that has metadata.design populated and status='in_progress' but no children at all — looks like task creation got interrupted last time
evals.expected_output: Recognizes epic-without-children pattern (design exists, no children). Enters Step 1b but skips epic creation (already exists). Creates tasks inline (no subagent), validates, finalizes with children array, falls through to dispatch.
expectations: Recognizes epic has design but no children — interrupted preparation.
Enters Step 1b, skips epic creation (already exists), creates tasks inline (no subagent), validates, adds children array, falls through to dispatch.

evals.prompt:
/develop t300

scope task is approved but metadata.design is empty string. metadata.plan_file is set to /tmp/scope-webhook.md though, and that file has the full phased design
evals.expected_output: Detects empty metadata.design, finds metadata.plan_file, reads the plan file via ct plan latest as fallback. Uses that content as the design source for preparation. Creates epic and tasks from the plan file content.
expectations: Detects empty design, reads plan_file as fallback. Does NOT ask about missing design when plan_file exists.
Uses plan file content as design source for Step 1b.

evals.prompt:
/develop

there's an orphaned epic — impl_team is set, status is in_progress. it has 5 children: 3 are completed (they modified src/webhooks/sender.ts, retry.ts, and types.ts), 2 are still pending
evals.expected_output: Enters recovery flow. Scans all children first. Finds 3 completed + 2 pending. Only dispatches the 2 pending children — the 3 completed children's work is preserved unconditionally (never reset, re-run, or modify them). After pending tasks complete, proceeds to Teardown.
expectations: Checks all children statuses before deciding recovery action.
Only dispatches the 2 pending children. Does NOT re-dispatch the 3 completed tasks.

evals.prompt:
/develop

orphaned epic again — impl_team set, in_progress. but all 4 children are completed. looks like the session crashed right before teardown
evals.expected_output: Recognizes all children are completed. Clears impl_team, skips directly to Teardown (acceptance + stage changes). Does NOT spawn any workers — all the work is already done.
expectations: All children completed → clears impl_team, skips to Teardown. No workers spawned.

evals.prompt:
/develop epic 500 webhook-retry

team mode, 3 leaf tasks. worker for task 501 just came back with:

"RESCOPE: The webhook retry approach assumes idempotent endpoints, but the payment service uses non-idempotent POST for charge creation. Retrying would double-charge customers."

tasks 502 and 503 haven't started yet
evals.expected_output: Detects RESCOPE signal from worker 501. Immediately halts all dispatch — does NOT start 502 or 503, does NOT retry 501. Invokes Skill('scope', '--continue <epicId>') to re-scope the design with the new constraint about non-idempotent endpoints.
expectations: Detects 'RESCOPE:' in worker output. Does NOT retry 501 or dispatch 502/503.

evals.prompt: /develop finished for epic 600 webhook-retry. acceptance passed. the spec says max 5 retry attempts but we actually implemented 3. also added circuit breaker logic that's not in the spec. metadata.spec_file is /tmp/scope-webhook-spec.md, and there's a copy at docs/specs/webhook-retry.md in the repo
evals.expected_output: After acceptance, dispatches a sonnet subagent with the spec + full git diff to reconcile. Subagent updates spec to match reality: max 3 attempts (not 5), adds circuit breaker behavior. Updated spec is written to metadata.spec, metadata.spec_file (/tmp/scope-webhook-spec.md), AND the repo copy (docs/specs/webhook-retry.md). Reconciled spec uses present-tense, no transition language like 'changed from 5 to 3'.
expectations: Dispatches reconciliation subagent with spec content + full git diff (not just file names).
Updates all three locations: metadata.spec, spec_file path, repo copy at docs/specs/webhook-retry.md.
Reconciled spec is present-tense ('max 3 attempts', 'circuit breaker monitors failure rate') — no transition language.

evals.prompt:
/develop

epic 700 cache-layer just finished — all 3 child tasks completed (t701: 'Add Redis cache client', t702: 'Cache invalidation on write', t703: 'Cache warm-up on deploy'). Teardown is done, about to stage changes. The 3 tasks produced 3 commits touching src/cache/client.ts, src/cache/invalidation.ts, src/cache/warmup.ts, and tests/cache.test.ts
evals.expected_output: Before staging, runs git diff --stat HEAD~3 and git rev-parse to get commit range. Derives summary from child subjects. Stores metadata.completion_summary on the epic with files_changed array, commit_range, and a one-line summary combining the three child subjects.
expectations: Runs git diff --stat HEAD~N where N = number of completed children (3). Collects file paths from the output.
Stores metadata.completion_summary with files_changed, commit_range (base..head SHAs), and a summary derived from child task subjects — not a generic description.
